items.title: CVE-2026-34926: Trend Micro Apex One Path Traversal Actively Exploited, CISA KEV Added
items.summary: A relative directory path traversal vulnerability in Trend Micro Apex One is being exploited in zero-day attacks in the wild. CISA added CVE-2026-34926 to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog and mandated federal civilian agencies patch by June 4, 2026.
items.why: Active zero-day exploitation with a federal patch deadline under 10 days away. Apex One is widely deployed in enterprise and government environments, making this a high-value target for initial access.

items.title: CVE-2026-42897 (CVSS 8.1): Microsoft Exchange Server RCE Triggered by Crafted Email
items.summary: Microsoft disclosed an actively exploited vulnerability in on-premise Exchange Server with a CVSS score of 8.1. The flaw can be triggered by delivering a specially crafted email to a target mailbox, requiring no authentication or user interaction beyond receipt.
items.why: Email-borne RCE with zero interaction is a critical threat vector. On-prem Exchange remains prevalent across enterprise and government networks, and active exploitation means patch windows are effectively closed.

items.title: APT28 Exploits CVE-2026-21509 Within 24 Hours to Target European Military & Government
items.summary: Russia-linked APT28 (Fancy Bear) weaponized a newly disclosed Microsoft Office vulnerability (CVE-2026-21509) within 24 hours of public disclosure in a stealthy multi-stage espionage campaign targeting European military and government entities, routing C2 traffic through cloud infrastructure to evade detection.
items.why: A sub-24-hour exploitation window confirms APT28's elite operational tempo. Cloud-based C2 complicates blocking via traditional network controls and highlights the risk of 1-day exploitation at nation-state speed.

items.title: Mandiant M-Trends 2026: eCrime Breakout Time Drops to 29 Minutes, 65% Faster Than 2024
items.summary: Mandiant's M-Trends 2026 report, synthesized from 500,000+ hours of incident response engagements across 2025, reveals the average eCrime breakout time collapsed to just 29 minutes. Global median dwell time rose to 14 days, and threat actors increasingly target backup infrastructure and identity services to deny recovery and force ransom payments.
items.why: A 29-minute breakout window means most organizations cannot detect, triage, and contain an intrusion before lateral movement begins — fundamentally undermining perimeter and alert-based defense strategies.

items.title: CrowdStrike 2026 Global Threat Report: Cloud Intrusions Surge 136%, AI Attacks Now Mainstream
items.summary: CrowdStrike's 2026 Global Threat Report documents a 136% surge in cloud environment intrusions year-over-year, mainstream adoption of generative AI by threat actors for phishing content and malware development, and a dramatic rise in vishing attacks as adversaries bypass MFA and endpoint controls through voice-based social engineering.
items.why: The cloud intrusion spike signals a fundamental attacker pivot from endpoints to cloud identity and control planes. The vishing trend directly undermines MFA — the most common enterprise defense against credential-based attacks.
